data.title: Microservices Architecture
data.description: Netflix employs a microservices architecture that allows for independent deployment and scaling of services.
data.technical_spec: Each service is independently deployable and integrates using REST or message queues.

data.title: Distributed Data Management
data.description: Data is distributed across various global data centers to ensure low latency and high availability.
data.technical_spec: Utilizes NoSQL databases like Cassandra and Dynomite.

data.title: Content Delivery Network (CDN)
data.description: Netflix uses its proprietary CDN, Open Connect, to stream content efficiently worldwide.
data.technical_spec: CDN nodes are strategically located near ISPs to optimize content delivery.

data.title: Scalable Cloud Infrastructure
data.description: Netflix operates entirely on the cloud using AWS, ensuring scalability and reliability.
data.technical_spec: Utilizes AWS services such as EC2, S3, and DynamoDB.

data.title: Resilient System Design
data.description: Systems are designed to be fault-tolerant and resilient against server failures.
data.technical_spec: Implements circuit breakers and fallback protocols.
